com.hp.workpath.api.webservices

Class WebServices

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.hp.workpath.api.webservices.WebServices

  • public class WebServices
extends java.lang.Object
    Class for managing WebServices. Webservice needs to extend AbstractWebServices and register its Callback so that the service could handle GET/PUT/POST/DELETE methods with authenticated method. Note: WebService does not support concurrent processing so you should not run heavy work that takes too long time to be done on a method.
    Since:
    API 6

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description

      static boolean isSupported(android.content.Context context)
      This method is needed to determine if the service supported or not.
      static void register(android.content.Context context, WebServicesAttributes attributes)

      • WebServices

        public WebServices()

    • Method Detail

      • register

        public static void register(android.content.Context context,
                            WebServicesAttributes attributes)
        Method to register the webservice
        Parameters:
        context - The Context in which the application is running.
        attributes - Object for WebServicesAttributes builder
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Calling a method on a null reference(context or attributes) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 6

      • isSupported

        public static boolean isSupported(android.content.Context context)
        This method is needed to determine if the service supported or not. If it's not supported, Webservice operation will be failed.
        Parameters:
        context - The Context in which the application is running.
        Returns:
        result of supported. If service is supported, method returns true. Otherwise method returns false.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Calling a method on a null reference(context) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 6
